text: Lesson 37
zh: 第37课

text: The Westhaven Express
zh: 威斯特黑文快车

text: What was the mistake the author made?
zh: 作者犯了什么样的错误？

text: We have learnt to expect that trains will be punctual.
zh: 我们已经习惯了指望火车准点。

text: After years of conditioning, most of us have developed an unshakable faith in railway timetables.
zh: 多年的训练使我们大多数人对火车时刻表产生了不可动摇的信任。

text: Ships may be delayed by storms;
zh: 轮船可能因风暴而延误；

text: flights may be cancelled because of bad weather; but trains must be on time.
zh: 航班可能因恶劣天气而取消；但火车必须准点。

text: Only an exceptionally heavy snow fall might temporarily dislocate railway services.
zh: 只有异常大的降雪才可能暂时打乱铁路运营。

text: It is all too easy to blame the railway authorities when something does go wrong.
zh: 一旦出了差错，人们总是很容易责怪铁路当局。

text: The truth is that when mistakes occur, they are more likely to be ours than theirs.
zh: 事实是，出错的往往是我们自己，而不是他们。

text: After consulting my railway timetable, I noted with satisfaction that there was an express train to Westhaven.
zh: 查阅了火车时刻表后，我满意地发现有一趟去威斯特黑文的快车。

text: It went direct from my local station and the journey lasted a mere hour and seventeen minutes.
zh: 它从我当地的车站直达，全程只需一小时十七分钟。

text: When I boarded the train, I could not help noticing that a great many local people got on as well.
zh: 上车时，我不禁注意到有许多当地人也上了车。

text: At the time, this did not strike me as odd.
zh: 当时我并不觉得这有什么奇怪。

text: I reflected that there must be a great many people besides myself who wished to take advantage of this excellent service.
zh: 我想，除了我之外，一定还有很多人也想乘坐这趟出色的班车。

text: Neither was I surprised when the train stopped at Widley, a tiny station a few miles along the line.
zh: 火车在几英里外一个叫维德利的小站停下时，我也没觉得惊讶。

text: Even a mighty express train can be held up by signals.
zh: 即便是强大的快车，也可能被信号灯拦下。

text: But when the train dawdled at station after station, I began to wonder.
zh: 可是当火车一站接一站地磨蹭时，我开始起了疑心。

text: It suddenly dawned on me that this express was not roaring down the line at ninety miles an hour, but barely chugging along at thirty.
zh: 我猛然意识到，这趟所谓的快车并不是以每小时九十英里的速度疾驰，而是以每小时三十英里的速度缓缓爬行。

text: One hour and seventeen minutes passed and we had not even covered half the distance.
zh: 一小时十七分钟过去了，我们连一半的路程都没走完。

text: I asked a passenger if this was the Westhaven Express, but he had not even heard of it.
zh: 我问一位乘客这是不是威斯特黑文快车，他却连听都没听说过。

text: I determined to lodge a complaint as soon as we arrived.
zh: 我下定决心，一到站就要投诉。

text: Two hours later, I was talking angrily to the station master at Westhaven.
zh: 两小时后，我正在威斯特黑文车站愤怒地跟站长理论。

text: When he denied the train's existence, I borrowed his copy of the timetable.
zh: 他矢口否认有这趟车，于是我借来他的一份时刻表。

text: There was a note of triumph in my voice when I told him that it was there in black and white.
zh: 我用一种得意的语气告诉他，白纸黑字写得清清楚楚。

text: Glancing at it briefly, he told me to look again.
zh: 他扫了一眼，让我再仔细看看。

text: A tiny asterisk conducted me to a footnote at the bottom of the page.
zh: 一个小小的星号把我引到了页面底部的一条注释。

text: It said: 'This service has been suspended.'
zh: 上面写着：“此班次已停运。”
